title: Furnished short-term rentals for medical residents in Cleveland
metaTitle: Furnished Rentals for Medical Residents in Cleveland
metaDescription: Starting a residency at Cleveland Clinic or UH? See how furnished short-term rentals work near Cleveland's hospitals, then tell us what you need.
answerBox: Medical residents starting at Cleveland Clinic or University Hospitals typically look for furnished, short-term rentals near the Fairfax and University Circle areas, close to both institutions, with a lease length matched to a rotation or the first year of residency rather than a standard 12-month term. Availability and furnishing vary property by property.

sections.h2: What do medical residents need in a rental near Cleveland's hospitals?
body: Cleveland's two largest employers are healthcare systems — **Cleveland Clinic**, the region's top employer, and **University Hospitals (UH)**, the county's second-largest — per Crain's Cleveland Business's 2025 largest-employers data. Residents relocating for a program at either system generally want three things a standard year-long unfurnished lease doesn't always offer: furniture already in place, a lease length that can match a rotation schedule rather than locking into a full year, and reasonable proximity to campus for early call shifts.

Not every rental home is set up this way — furnished units and short-term terms are the exception, not the rule, in most Cleveland rental listings, so it's worth confirming both details directly with a landlord or property manager before assuming they're available.

sections.h2: Where are Cleveland Clinic and UH relative to the neighborhoods with rental homes?
body: Cleveland Clinic's main campus sits in the **Fairfax** neighborhood, east of Downtown; University Hospitals' main campus is in **University Circle**, home to Case Western Reserve University and several cultural institutions, and served by the Red Line rail plus the HealthLine bus-rapid-transit route along Euclid Avenue. **Hough**, between Downtown and University Circle, and **Glenville**, just north of University Circle, both sit within a short commute of both hospital systems.
We manage rental homes in several of these East Cleveland neighborhoods, including Fairfax (44104), Hough (44103), and Glenville (44108) — all within the general vicinity of both hospital campuses.

sections.h2: Furnished vs. unfurnished, short-term vs. standard lease
body: The tradeoffs are straightforward: a furnished, short-term rental costs more per month than an unfurnished 12-month lease at a comparable address, because the landlord is absorbing furniture costs and turnover risk over a shorter commitment. A standard unfurnished lease is cheaper monthly but requires the resident to buy or bring furniture and typically commit to a full year.

rows: Matched to rotation length or under 12 months
Lower monthly rate, requires own furniture

sections.h2: Timing your search around Match Day and orientation
body: Because residency start dates are fixed by Match Day and program orientation schedules, most incoming residents have a narrow window to secure housing before relocating — often while still finishing a prior program out of state. That timeline makes remote scheduling important: ask any Cleveland landlord or property manager whether they can arrange a video walkthrough or a single in-person visit scheduled around a trip to sign a lease, rather than requiring multiple return visits before move-in.

It's also worth confirming move-in timing against your specific rotation start date in writing, since a gap of even a few days between when a unit is ready and when your program begins can mean paying for temporary housing on top of your new lease.

sections.h2: What we can offer incoming residents
body: We manage 90+ rental homes across Greater Cleveland, concentrated in East and Southeast neighborhoods — including Fairfax, Hough, and Glenville, all within reach of Cleveland Clinic and University Hospitals — and every home we manage accepts Section 8 / Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready. Furnishing and lease-length options vary by property, so the most reliable way to find out what's currently available is to [tell our team your program start date and rotation length](/p/book-showing) and we'll match you against current inventory.

You can also browse [houses for rent across Cleveland](/houses-for-rent-cleveland-oh/) or [apartments for rent in Cleveland](/apartments-for-rent-cleveland-oh/) to compare unfurnished options if a standard lease works better for your budget.

faq.q: Do you offer furnished rentals for medical residents in Cleveland?
faq.a: Furnishing and short-term lease options vary by individual property in our portfolio rather than being available on every home. Tell our team your program start date, rotation length, and hospital system, and we'll let you know what's currently available that fits.

faq.q: Which Cleveland neighborhoods are closest to Cleveland Clinic and University Hospitals?
faq.a: Cleveland Clinic's main campus is in Fairfax; University Hospitals' main campus is in University Circle. Hough and Glenville both sit within a short commute of both. We manage rental homes in Fairfax (44104), Hough (44103), and Glenville (44108).

faq.q: Can I get a short-term lease instead of a full year as a resident?
faq.a: Some properties can accommodate a shorter term matched to a rotation or the first year of residency, but this isn't standard across every unit. Confirm lease length directly with the property manager before assuming it's available, since most Cleveland rentals default to a 12-month term.

faq.q: Do your rentals near the hospitals accept Section 8 vouchers?
faq.a: Yes. Every home Rent Finder Cleveland manages, including those in Fairfax, Hough, and Glenville near Cleveland Clinic and University Hospitals, accepts Section 8 / Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready.
